etymology.description: Depicts a stone beneath a cliff (厂) -> distorted to 丆 -> stone
etymology.interpretation: The character 石(shí, 'stone') originally depicted a stone under a cliff, representing the idea of a solid, natural object found at the base of rocky formations.

examples.chinese: 那个花园里有一块很大的石头(那個花園裡有一塊很大的石頭)。
examples.pinyin: Nàge huāyuán lǐ yǒu yīkuài hěn dà de shítou.
examples.english: There is a big stone in the garden.

examples.chinese: 石头变得很滑，因为下雨了(石頭變得很滑，因為下雨了)。
examples.pinyin: Shítou biàn de hěn huá, yīnwèi xià yǔ le.
examples.english: The stone became very slippery because it rained.

examples.chinese: 他收集了很多不同种类的石头(他收集了很多不同種類的石頭)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shōujíle hěnduō bùtóng zhǒnglèi de shítou.
examples.english: He collected many different kinds of stones.
